title: HMM as PIT-safe regime labeler -- alternative to rule-based regimes.py
source: Conversation 2026-05-27: user asked whether Hidden Markov Models are compatible with the project. Higher-effort, higher-risk of the two HMM ideas.
why: pipeline/regimes.py labels regimes with a deterministic 2-axis rule (trend ROC+MA / vol with hysteresis) -> 4 classes. An HMM would learn transition/emission structure and could produce smoother, data-driven regime boundaries with probabilistic transitions instead of hand-tuned hysteresis + min-dwell. The risk is severe: an HMM is FITTED (Baum-Welch), so a global fit leaks future info into past labels -- a silent lookahead leak of exactly the kind the spec exists to prevent (cf. FRED .shift(1), PurgedTimeSeriesSplit, FrozenEstimator). Only worth pursuing if re-estimation is strictly expanding-window / online and reproducibility (seed, local-optima stability) is proven.
value_rationale: M, not H: the current rule-based labeler is honest-by-construction and already works. An HMM is a lateral move whose payoff (probabilistic regime) is speculative while its leak surface is large. Value is in the methodology comparison as much as any edge.
scope: Spec the PIT contract first: HMM re-fit per walk-forward fold on train-only data; no full-series Baum-Welch ever. Document how this maps onto make_folds + the regime_gate.
Determinism: fix init strategy + random_seed=42; quantify local-optima sensitivity across seeds; gate acceptance on stable labels.
Map HMM hidden states onto anonymized regime IDs and rebuild the quantile dossier so the hypothesizer firewall is preserved (states must carry no date semantics).
Head-to-head vs. rule-based regimes.py: episode counts, dwell, sanity_report bounds, and downstream audit outcomes on the same proposals.
Adversarial lookahead review of the HMM labeler code (custom fitted code is NOT auto-trusted), mirroring the B0040 custom-primary materialization gate philosophy.
out_of_scope: Using an HMM as a primary entry signal -- forbidden by the 'primaries are deterministic rule-based functions, not fitted classifiers' invariant (the 'squeeze the orange twice' failure case).

history.reason: Captured from HMM-compatibility discussion; flagged high lookahead-leak risk, gated on PIT re-fit design.
